description: A string slice, the most primitive string type in Rust. It is usually seen in its borrowed form &str. Unlike String, a &str is a reference to an existing UTF-8 encoded string and does not own its data. Python equivalent: str (but immutable and borrowed).

methods.name: len
methods.description: Returns the length of the string slice in bytes (not characters). For ASCII strings, this equals the number of characters.

methods.name: is_empty
methods.description: Returns true if the string slice has a length of zero.

methods.name: contains
methods.signature: fn contains<P: Pattern>(&self, pat: P) -> bool
methods.description: Returns true if the given pattern matches a sub-slice of this string. Python equivalent: 'world' in 'hello world'.

methods.name: starts_with
methods.signature: fn starts_with<P: Pattern>(&self, pat: P) -> bool
methods.description: Returns true if this string slice begins with the given pattern. Python equivalent: str.startswith().

methods.name: ends_with
methods.signature: fn ends_with<P: Pattern>(&self, pat: P) -> bool
methods.description: Returns true if this string slice ends with the given pattern. Python equivalent: str.endswith().

methods.name: split
methods.signature: fn split(&self, pat: P) -> Split
methods.description: Returns an iterator over substrings separated by the given pattern. Python equivalent: str.split().

methods.name: trim
methods.description: Returns a string slice with leading and trailing whitespace removed. Python equivalent: str.strip().

methods.name: to_uppercase
methods.description: Returns a new String with all characters converted to uppercase. Python equivalent: str.upper().

methods.name: chars
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the characters (char values) of the string slice. Use this to iterate character-by-character.

methods.name: bytes
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the bytes of the string slice. Each element is a u8.
